final_output.final_deliverable:
1) Axolotl neoteny — scientific name: **Ambystoma mexicanum**
- Evidence (quote): The Axolotl page’s taxobox lists the “**Binomial name**” as “**Ambystoma mexicanum**”.
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Axolotl

2) Voynich manuscript — currently held in: **Yale University’s Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library**
- Evidence (quote): “**Since 1969, it has been held in Yale University’s Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.**”
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voynich_manuscript

3) Pando clonal colony — US state located in: **Utah**
- Evidence (quote): “Pando … is the name of a quaking aspen … clone located in **Sevier County, Utah, United States** …”
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pando_(tree)

4) Antikythera mechanism — approximately what century BCE built: **2nd century BC (2nd century BCE)**
- Evidence (quote): The Antikythera mechanism page lists “**Created 2nd century BC**”.
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antikythera_mechanism

5) Mantis shrimp vision — approximate number of photoreceptor cell types: **between 12 and 16 types**
- Evidence (quote): “Compared with the four types of photoreceptor cell that humans possess in their eyes, the eyes of a mantis shrimp have **between 12 and 16 types of photoreceptor cells**.”
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mantis_shrimp

6) Svalbard Global Seed Vault — country located in: **Norway**
- Evidence (quote): In the infobox, the location is listed as “Spitsbergen, Longyearbyen, **Norway**”.
- Citation: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Svalbard_Global_Seed_Vault
final_output.action_summary: Answers (with Wikipedia URLs): 1) Ambystoma mexicanum (Axolotl). 2) Yale University’s Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library (Voynich manuscript). 3) Utah (Pando). 4) 2nd century BC/BCE (Antikythera mechanism). 5) ~12–16 photoreceptor types (Mantis shrimp). 6) Norway (Svalbard Global Seed Vault).
